I've never been to New Orleans, so I've had some fun experiences. Some of my teammates from here have been able to show me around and talk to me a little bit about the area. So, it's been fun and with them able to come back home, it's been a nice feeling for us and them. I've never been to the Superdome either. The overall construction of it, it's crazy and it's a lovely dome. We've been trying to emulate our midseason practices because that's what it takes to win these types of games. You need to have those types of practices, really physical, fast practices to get our bodies right and mentality right. So, that's been our motto. Hard, physical practices and we won't change from what we've been doing which has ultimately led us to where we are today. Everything's not going to be perfect, but that gave us motivation, you know? It flipped a switch in not only me, but my teammates and I think that showed throughout the rest of the season. We've gotten progressively better, and I think we still haven't played our best football yet. It's a great feeling because Coach Sarkisian says all the time that we can win any type of way. We're a versatile team whether it's offense or defense, special teams, running game, or passing game. All of us know that too and all of us know that we're playmakers. We do this for each other. We don't get jealous. If one person's number gets called and mine doesn't, I know they'll make their plays and the way they play is fine by me. It's a great feeling knowing that we have all these weapons and that we're so close as brothers. We're family and it's a great feeling. It would be a great feeling, not only for me, but our seniors. Sending them off with a National Championship would be a great feeling. It would be something legendary in Austin being able to bring that back to Austin since I was maybe two years old. Being able to do that and bringing that back to the city would be an amazing feeling.

Eyes and control, basically just folloiwng PK's plan. It's been amazing, he's a great coach and he puts eveyone in the right position...all we have to do is just pay attention. I want to give a big shout out to Coach Joseph just putting me in position and being able to do film with him throughout the week. I try to get extra film and try to hit on plays I want to steal and I try to look at the weaknesses. Obviously they have coaches and they’re trying to find out the weakness of myself, but I want to fix my weaknesses and I try to use my strengths come Saturday. So that was one of the plays that I put in my book and my coach told me I had to bait him kind of like Floyd Mayweather does. Floyd Mayweather doesn't just go in the fight trying to look for the knockout, he kind of builds and he guides and and kind of round five, he figures them out and then he throws his counter punches and stuff like that. So they ran that play on the fourth play of the game I believe and I played a different concept. It was the same coverage, but I showed a different alignment. And they came back to the play three plays later and I changed my alignment again and I was blessed to turn and the ball was right there Just the faith that I have. God's put me in this position. I've been through crazy things in life, but knowing that He’s got me here has been amazing; I've been through hella stuff but I could rely on God anytime in any moment. It doesn't matter what I'm going through and where I'm at,o I just put my trust in him and I put my trust in film, and then once again my coaches and my teammates that trust me to go out there and be aside them They have a great quarterback, great receivers, their coaching and their play scheme is amazing. But what we have to do as a defense is it's all about eye control. They give these different looks and they want us to slip up…so this is about just doing your job, all 11 on the field just doing your job and everybody has their own responsibility. It's been a blessing; it's still crazy to me. I go out there and run out there everytime and and I just look at the 100K, like it's a blessing, something I dreamed about when I was little, just live out this experience I can't take anything for granted There's been ups and downs throughout the years, but first, everyone on the team and I are blessed for the opportunity to be here and to be able to play another game for the fans and the University. God's blessed us with this opportunity to go out there and have fun against a competitive team. It's going to be a great experience. We've grown in the way that we have been paying attention and going about practice. In practice we try to "execute to a T." We obviously like to make practice harder than games for when the games come. Also, just following coach Pete Kwiatkowski's plan, knowing that this is his third year. We put our trust in him, and the results have been showing. And a big shout out to our defensive line. They've been killing it all year. That's been a big positive. They help us in the back end with the timing, putting pressure on the quarterback, and things like that. Everything's just a piece of the puzzle. But it just starts off with coach Kwiatkowski, following his plan. They are an explosive team with an explosive offense. They have a great quarterback, a great wide receiver core group, and offensive line. But the thing that will get them going is shots. They thrive off shots and want to catch the defense slipping. They try to do a lot of formations just to trick teams in the back end for having miscommunications, wide open people, offsides, and things of that sort. We just must stay mentally engaged and not worry about the outside noise or get caught up in what type of game it is. At the end of the day, it is just a game. The yards on the field and the color of the field are not changing. We just have to go out there and have fun and play as a team and a family like we have been doing. I mean, this starts all like during the summer. Our culture Wednesdays and again just opening up to one another, but I wish Sark could have had a card. We do these culture things on I would say Friday, but today is not Friday. So we do it Tuesday. Tuesday night, we do a team activity, and we write in the card. And in the card, I wrote just thanking Sark and the last bullet point was Quinn is going to play fast and the reason he's going to play fast is because of God the creator. He's going to give all his worries and everything he has and give it to him and allow him to play free. And I think that's what Quinn did. When our back is against the wall, he just kept fighting and made some big-time plays in those three throws. Quinn's a leader. He's been leading us this whole time. We used to be roommates. He used to bully me telling me go take out the trash, screaming at me go to sleep. Just Quinn, he strives to be perfect, but he wants everybody else to be perfect. But, again, he knows the Lord is his creator and he can give everything to him. He can just go play fast, literally go out there and play fast. We're all blessed to be here and blessed to play a game we love, but at the end of the day, you can't make a mistake in God's eyes. As a competitor, you know, it's fun. It's fun when guys say what they say before the game and that's just his confidence level and things like that. But just to go out there with my guys and keep fighting. I think we kept fighting. Sometimes if we had a penalty and they got another drive, another play, we just continued to fight. And I think ultimately that's what it means and that's what brotherhood means here and the culture and level of bond that we have and the level of bond that we share. Sark told us at halftime, this was the point where we were at last year, and we didn't handle business and just to be able to have another opportunity to go out there and handle business, that was a blessing to do that. So him just continuing to just fight us and push us through that adversity and things like that. I mean, yesterday in Bible study with Kevin Washington, there was a few guys that went up there. Kevin asked us to tell the reason why you deeply and strongly got closer with God and why you trust God, and Cedric went up there and he told us his story, and Jake Majors went up there, and I also went up there. I told all the guys like last year and the years before previously, I wanted things done on my time. But it doesn't work like that. God has everything planned. It's already written. And all you have to do is just keep going with the process and just trusting the process and then allowing everybody to be a plus in your life and trusting everybody. So just literally relying on God. And Psalms 105:1, just give thanks to the Lord and proclaim His greatness and show the whole world what He has done. I mean, I'm showing you all my talent, but I'll be surprised, too, when God just pushes me to go out there and allows me to make plays like that. It meant everything to share even the stage with Drew and Taaffe for that moment, and it meant everything for Bam back then to just push us, not even knowing that we were going to be at this point of life. But push us to want to see great things out of us. He played at a high level, and honestly just to build a brotherhood, you know. He's a role model to me. He's a big brother to me, but he pushed me and Taaffe and Drew to a point that we thought we could never be pushed before, just having us train every single day. So I think it allowed us to have a better bond obviously me, Drew and Taaffe with us being on the same team now to back then, too. I mean, I wouldn't say you don't have any help. I always say there is help, with out of bounds, things like that. And then just knowing where everybody is at on the field. If I have a linebacker kind of flexed out at the apex position, you know, if the post is leaning towards me, just little things; just always playing with my leverage. But at the end of the day it's mano e mano, so just playing my technique and kind of understanding what routes I can get, what's the route tree, what way did he steer me, and just playing with good leverage. I think that's the honest standpoint. That's what I try to do. Yeah, that's my brother. We actually — I mean, throughout the whole season we chat literally every week, except this week. But, nah, we're always chatting. I'm always reaching out to him; he's always reaching out to me and things like that. Just motivation to keep us going. He's been through so much. I've been through so much. We started here together as a brother, and that's always going to be my brother. I'm just proud of him and how far he's came. He's dealt with a lot of issues, just injuries and having to sit out. So I'm just proud that he has an opportunity to showcase the gift that God gave him. So it's always good to see that out of him. You know, actually me and PK, we were chatting about something at practice, one of the plays he had called in the game. I'm not going to give away the details of how he executes things, but I would say he's aggressive, you know. He's so patient. Sometimes you see us moving around a little bit late, but he's just trying to make the right call to put us in the right position. So hats off to him just being so patient with the call and being confident in what he makes. I've been in situations sometimes where you could try to rush to get the call and things like that and you get the call very fast, and then we're just kind of playing within the call and I wouldn't say having us play like robots. But this year we're just most definitely just playing free. He allows us to play free with the call he makes, and he's always putting us in position to win. I tell y'all all the time, if we don't make the play, that's our fault. That's not PK's fault. He's putting us in the right position. We just have to go out there and execute it. Things like that can't happen, especially me and Taaffe just growing up here, just seeing what it was like when we were little, seeing all the great DBs that came here and that created a history for us, you know. And then it's our job to honor the ones that came before us and leave a legacy for the ones after us. So just throughout the summer we have to clean up details in our game, tools in our game. But also just on a group standpoint, just being better communicating just trusting one another. But that started off our coaches on Wednesdays just trusting one another off the field so we can trust each other on the field. So I think that will play a big factor just coaching on Wednesdays just allowing us to — I know my brother is going to be there and he knows I'm going to be in position. So I think that's what helped us. I mean, honestly, my body is about the same to any other season. You know, just not dealing with any injuries, so that's good. But other than that, like just the soreness, and I mean, it's about the same. You just have to stay in the treatment room, the normal things. But I wouldn't say it's more taxing. They gave us a good amount of bye weeks during the season, so I think that's what helped a lot. And especially that last bye week, that helped a lot just getting our bodies back and things like that. Just you always have to be on your game. You can't be relaxed. I feel sometimes we could get relaxed as a team or just we've been playing so high. Never be so high, never be too low. Just always stay ready to go and never let the moment get too big, you know. And you always have to just execute no matter the stage, no matter the game, no matter the play and just give the player respect to each play that you're coming across. I mean you can't recreate three awesome legends. But I mean, no, they're going to be good. Jelani, he's for sure a guy that I see just taking the role of leading the back group. He's so smart and he's learning behind Taaffe and Drew. So that's amazing for him and just his athletic ability to move around, to be everywhere on the field. He still plays nickel and safety and things like that. So his flexibility, I know that's going to be amazing. And I know Jiggy, No. 23; he's going to be a heck of a player for us, too. He can play the nickel. He can play the safety and things like that. And then Colby at the corner. More of the boundary with the lint and stuff like that. He's learning from Coach Joseph. They're going to be a good group of guys, but I for sure see Lani just taking control and taking that leader role. It's all based off of him. We have certain things where we could tweak it as players and we can kind of do our own thing out there. But just him being patient and allowing — we're waiting for him to see what he sees, and then he's going to give his best intake on what he sees and it's going to allow us to just kind of play fast. But we kind of mix it up and we can fix things out there, structure of the defense with Taaffe fixing things to Drew or me or the linebackers. It all plays hand in hand. They're amazing, they have an amazing coach and you can tell they play for one another. You can tell when they turn on tape. He runs the ball — you don't see teams blocking down, receivers blocking down the field 40 yards all the time. That's not normal. So they play for one another and you can tell they love him and they love each other as a team. And they're playing physical. But as him as a back, he's so patient. He's patient in the backfield and he trusts his line. When he sees something, when he sees a hole, he hits it and he hits it downhill. He's not looking to juke. He has an amazing jump cut to him and he's doing the jump cut to get down the field. So it's going to be an amazing opportunity against him and his team. I think honestly back then it helped us to start being leaders back then. We used to lead one another. We used to get on each other's ass and things like that to push each other to get to the point where we are today. When we messed up and made a mistake, Taaffe or Drew, they'll be getting on me. If I drop the ball, they would make me do push ups. So things like that it allowed us to get to this point now. And for me and Mukuba and Taaffe to be leaders on the back end and for me and Taaffe to be leaders on this team, I think it all started honestly back then when we went through those sessions when it was just five of us just pushing each other to be great and pushing excellence out of one another. So I think that's what it helped with.

You know, I thought it was just really good for us to go out there and kind of you know show all the hard work we've been putting [in] especially this offseason you know. We didn't finish the season how we wanted to last year and I thought just getting an opportunity game two to go to Tuscaloosa and kind of just, you know, prove ourselves and kind of step up to the moment, I thought it was a really great experience. Definitely, when we got back uh there's a video out there of a lot of the students. We got a tower here on campus and a lot of the students were there celebrating the win and they kind of waited for us to get back off our flight and get back off the bus and we all went up there and they congratulated us and took pictures with us and it was just really cool to see all the pride that all the students had and just all-around, the university, making them proud. It means a lot. Being a guy that's from Texas, being able to represent the University and being able to represent the state as a whole, I think just getting a chance to go out there and play in this historic rivalry, it means a lot to me. I know it means a lot to the guys as well, so I'm really excited I think that game, for a lot of people, was a big stage liek you siad. And for us to go out there and play like we did there, gave us a boost of confidence for the entire team and the entire program. I think right now we're just kind of driving with that confidence. Gotta keep building on it and here we go, another big game for us, another team that's undefeated, another challenge. So we just gotta go out there, execute, and accept the challenge. I think it starts in the offseason. From the moment, not to be funny, but ever since he cut his hair, I feel like he had a different mentality. I think he wanted to mature his physical look. I'm really proud of the growth he's made, and his ability to come out here and ignore critics and what everyone says and just execute week in and week out? Quinn's a jokester when you least expect it. He's always kinda, especially when I talk to him, a really funny guy. Last game, we did a little media, we were on the radio talking. The whole time I'm talking, he's looking at me, and there's other people in the room, but he's just giving me this look like just staring at me with a little funny face, and he's got the whole room laughing. I'm trying to keep a straight face and talk on the radio, but that just shows the kind of guy he is. He's not always serious and quiet, as he may give off. But you know, I think that's just him and I really love him. I think our mentality has just been understanding what we've been trying to achieve this whole year, and then going out there and actually doing it. Do what it takes to go out there and play at a high level. Everybody understands that they have a role on this team, and I think everyone's done a good job to take responsibility for their own role, to come out each week, practice however they need to and get better every week. I think the biggest part is realizing how far you've come. Realizing you didn't come this far just to be here and enjoy the moment; you came this far to go win a national championship and hoist that trophy up into the air. I think when you keep that at the forefront of your mind, it pushes you to be better and to keep driving until you get to that point. Honestly, just the experience of being with my teammates. Last year we had the Alamo Bowl, so this (Allstate Sugar Bowl) is a big step up. It's kind of a little vacation for the guys, but it's still business with the game and all. For us to get away from Austin and have a trip together and play ball, you really couldn't ask for much more. The mindset of the team is pretty good right now. I think for the most part everybody understood what their role is and what their job is for the team. Everybody knows what we need to do to go and win so I think now it is just making sure that we clean up the mistakes that we made since we started preparing and have some fun and enjoy this time that we have and then go out there and handle business. I would say not being satisfied is one of the things that keeps driving us. Winning the Big 12 championship was a big accomplishment for us, but when we started the season, we wanted more than that and we wanted to achieve more than that. I think for us it was just a part of the process and a part of our journey. We understand what we really want to go and achieve, which is a National Championship, and now that we have gotten a chance to compete for one, I think nobody wants it more than the guys on this team. First and foremost, the one loss we had in the season to Oklahoma. I don't think that was something extremely hard to overcome, just we didn't want to lose especially that game being that it was a rivalry. Our response from there on out really showed the strength and the mental toughness of this team. We were able to go out there week in and week out and continue, even when games are close to pull out on top. We look back at it and wish we could have that game back, but we are in the position that we are now, so I don't think anyone really holds that much to it and looking to go win a Natty now.

Man it means a lot for this team, it means a lot for me individually, it just means so much. There's a lot of people that critique us on what we do but like I said, I posted it the other day like man we’re 10-1. I haven't been 10-1 since high school and that was you know decades ago so I'm just really happy, really grateful, really blessed to be in this position and I'm really happy that we won this game. It's my first time winning in this stadium. I would say, number one, is just I've been through a lot of stuff, so I really do not take any play for granted. I kind of feel like I try to play every play like it's my last. Sometimes I’m tired, but for the most part, man I'm out there to just help this team win. I came back to win a Big 12 title and to win a Big 12 title, you have to have those types of plays. I'm just not selfish, I'm just out here to win. If I wanted to play by myself, I would have went and played golf or something…it's a team sport. I mean we’re celebrating this win you know tonight. We're going to move on and we know next week, we got a big one at senior night. We got to send the seniors out with a big one. It's my last home game at DKR for real this time, so we're just really excited you know? Other than that, it’s just the next step, we have a mission and we’re excited to get to it. Yeah I definitely was able to speak to him. I'm not sure if people know, but Jonathan and [T’Vondre] Sweat are my best friends on the team, so when that happened, I felt for him. I'm actually a person that's been through it. He knows that. He knows my story; we're from the same area. I came to him and we talked about it. I told him if he ever needs any advice because I know how hard it is, especially in his position. I know that must have been really tough, but it wasn't just me that rallied around him, we had the whole team do that. And you can see that he's really positive on the sideline. He's coaching the guys up and all the younger running backs look up to him. He's the best running back in the country, so when he tells them something they listen. Yeah, he gave us a culture you know? When you have a culture and he instilled love in this team and we all truly love each other. And when you love somebody, you're willing to do stuff; you're willing to be a receiver and go block a linebacker; you're willing to be a quarterback and chase the ball down…if it fumbles, you'll jump on it you know? You're willing to do stuff that normal teams won't do if they're not really connected and I think that's the biggest thing. We've always had the talent and now we're a five star culture with five-star players. You just have to give the game the respect it deserves. All the external things are really cool, so we'll get to see it and experience it, but other than that you have to respect the game because it demands it and we are here to win. We are here to play a football game. At the end of the day, you have to figure out what you want more, and we all know what we want, which is to play a football game. The other stuff like the city is cool, but we are locked in. It's human nature to remember that you are not just going to forget that game, but at the same time teams change throughout the years so we are playing a new team and they are playing a new team so we are just sticking to how we prepare. It will be exciting that's a new team, really good, and we've got a lot of respect for them. It will be fun. Today is when I'm going to do all the eating and then after this I am going to taper down. New Orleans has got something coming because I am coming for the food. I want some gumbo; I want everything that I can only get here. When I travel, I only want the local stuff, that's what I am coming for today.

This year it's kind of different; it's kind of like I'm the guy needing to help the younger guys out, making sure I stay focused during practice, during walk-throughs, and just make sure I give good feedback to younger guys. My technique is definitely, I feel like, growing since last year. Flood has done a good job teaching everyone on the o-line the same techniques, making sure we know what stuff to take and what steps not to take, and not just telling us but telling us why we should take those steps. Oh yeah, I feel like it's good for the team. I feel like it's real good for the team because the first game is Rice; it's an early evening game, so I feel like that's helping us get ready and preparing for the first game of the season.
